Analyst (Management view) asked:
“FY 24 should be an excellent year for growth for us, and capacity
utilization will improve substantially.”
Water Tanks
On track to meet ₹100 cr FY23 revenue guidance.
Current tank margins already 12–13% at low utilization; steady-state margin target 14–15%.
Operating from five locations, more may be added as it is a logistics-driven business.
Adhesives & Paints
Financials & Margins
Adhesives + Paints Revenue Growth
Q3 FY23: +28.6% YoY
9M FY23: +39.7% YoY
(Not strictly comparable YoY because prior period did not include paints.)
EBITDA – Adhesives + Paints segment
Q2 FY23: 12.6%
Q3 FY23 reported: 12.2%
Impacted by ₹5.5 cr forex loss in UK (Seal It).
Adjusted for this, Q3 segment EBITDA ~13.8%.

Analyst (Management reiteration of philosophy) asked:
“Astral is believing in consistency of growth.”
Medium-term guidance unchanged: “we will be doubling our revenue in next five years”
implying 15%+ revenue CAGR.
“We are not a commodity company… we are a brand” and will not chase volumes at the cost
of margins.
Consolidated Financial Performance
Revenue Growth
Q3 FY23: +15% YoY.
9M FY23: +21.6% YoY.
EBITDA
Q3 FY23: down 9.5% YoY, impacted by high-cost PVC & chemical inventory and forex loss in
UK.
9M FY23: down 6.4% YoY for similar reasons.
Working Capital & Cash

Concall Summary - Nov 2022
Astral Limited – Q2 FY23 Earnings Call Summary (Nov 11, 2022)
Big Picture
• Quarter impacted by sharp PVC price correction (~Rs 30/kg in Q2; ~Rs 60+ over 6
months) leading to significant inventory losses, yet management highlighted that
Astral delivered “a very healthy double-digit EBITDA growth” and “perhaps…the
highest margin in the industry.”
• Consolidated revenue de-grew 2.4% YoY in Q2, but grew ~25–26% in H1.
• Management continues to emphasize sustainable growth + healthy margins,
reiterating that 15–17% pipe EBITDA margin is structurally achievable:
“Our company always believe in sustainable growth and reasonable margin.”
“We are not in a rat race…nor do we want to sacrifice our margins in lieu of volumes for a
short-term basis.”
Segment Performance & Outlook
1. Pipes & Fittings
Q2/H1 Performance
• Q2 plumbing business revenue de-grew ~10%, with 4% volume de-growth, mainly
due to:
• High base and channel de-stocking
• 6 days lost in Sept due to SAP data migration post-merger (Astral Pipes &
Resinova)
• Heavy PVC inventory loss.
• EBITDA margin for pipes: 13.4% in Q2, 14.2% in H1 (reported, depressed by PVC
inventory loss and rupee depreciation).
• Management estimate PVC inventory loss of ~Rs 70 crores in H1 (Q1+Q2), with
some residual hit expected in Q3:
“Very difficult to arrive exact inventory loss number…roughly about Rs.70 crores we have
lost in the first half…some loss may come in this Q3 also for PVC because PVC is still falling.”
Mix & Market Share

Concall Summary - Aug 2022
Astral Ltd – Q1 FY23 Earnings Call Summary (12 Aug 2022)
Operating Environment & Overall Commentary
• Management emphasized that Q1 was “full of challenges” with “very volatile”
markets and “rapidly dropping” polymer prices.
• Falling PVC prices led to dealers/distributors keeping “very lean inventory,” making
“month-on-month demand… very unrealistic to predict.”
• Despite this, Astral claims “one of the best growth” quarters on a consolidated basis,
with both plumbing and adhesives performing well, though margins were hit
by inventory losses and FX.
Financial Performance & One-offs
Segment Performance (Q1 FY23, YoY)
• Pipes / Plumbing (incl. tanks; excl. paints, faucets, sanitaryware)
• Revenue growth: +73%
• Volume growth: +48% (on a low Q1FY22 base)
• EBITDA growth: +30%
• Demand growth predominantly from plumbing, with limited agri exposure:
“We are predominantly a plumbing company.”
• Adhesives & Sealants (Resinova + Seal IT; excl. paints)
• Revenue growth: +45%
• EBITDA growth: +43%
• EBITDA margin: 13.6% vs 11% in Q4 FY22 (+260 bps), but “still lower than
our expected range of 15%.”
• Resinova India grew ~18%; UK (Seal IT) had “very muted growth of 4-5%” due
to constraints.
• Paints (Gem Paints – consolidated from Q1)
• Q1FY23: ~₹55 cr revenue, 15% EBITDA margin.
• Last year Q4 (indicative): ~₹50 cr revenue, ~14.5% EBITDA (~₹7 cr).
Consolidated Margin Headwinds

Analyst (Volume growth) asked:
9M FY23 pipe volumes already ~+20% YoY; FY23 expected to close with “very, very high
double-digit” volume growth.
Long-term volume guidance unchanged at 12–15% p.a.; FY24 likely better given low
utilization and completed capex.
EBITDA Margins – Pipes:
Q2 FY23: 13.5%
Q3 FY23: 15.4% reported

Analyst (Installed pipes capacity) asked:
286,095 MT.
9M FY23 utilization: ~57%.

Analyst (Attendee) asked:
And sir this value added would be now for like the full year would be how much percentage of our
revenue? And what do we see that let's say scaling up or is that something sensitive?

Management answered:
Full year?

Analyst (Management) asked:
“with this Dahej plant, the adhesive… business as a whole… can generate
revenue of close to about ₹1,800 crore” vs ~₹1,000 crore last year – a substantial capacity
addition.
Focus will be largely on consumer side of adhesives.
Paints (Gem Paints)
9M FY23 growth: ~15%.
Q3 FY23:

• Normal, sustainable pipe EBITDA: 15–17% range, vs recent 20–21% peak driven by
inventory gains:
“These are not sustainable margins…there is a sizeable element of inventory gain into that.”
• Will defend market share if needed:
“Even if we lose 1% or 2% margin, we are sitting with the highest industry margin…we will
fight.”
2. Tanks & Valves
• Water Tanks
• Running at Rs 7–8 crores quarterly run-rate.
• On track to cross Rs 100 crores of sales in FY23:
“We are maintaining that Rs.100 crores guidance.”
• Seasonally stronger in H2 post-monsoon.
• Valves
• New valve plant operational for plumbing and industrial valves.
• Launched 12 SKUs so far; plan to roll out another 108 SKUs in Q3 (total 120
SKUs), all value-added:
“From there, you can understand that it is going to help us to improve our margins also.”
3. Adhesives (Resinova + UK + others)
Performance
• Adhesives & paints combined grew ~27% in Q2 and ~46% in H1.
• Segment EBITDA margin: 13.4% in Q2, 14.4% in H1.
• Within adhesives:
• Resinova + UK margins around 13%.
• UK top line impacted by GBP depreciation vs INR (approx. 8% revenue hit).
Outlook
• Raw material costs (chemicals) have started to fall:
• Management expects 15%+ EBITDA margin in adhesives from FY24:

Analyst (Net working capital days) asked:
34

Analyst (Receivable days) asked:
23

• Full production from early FY24.
• UK operations – “good growth and are unable to fulfill the demand”;
management is evaluating CAPEX allocation to expand capacity for UK and US
markets.
New Business Verticals & Strategic Direction
From Q1FY23, Astral is recasting its reporting into two verticals:
1. Plumbing vertical
• Includes: Pipes, Water Tanks, Faucets, Sanitaryware, and (for segment
reporting) Paints.
2. Adhesives & Paints vertical
• Includes: Gem Paints, Resinova, and Seal IT (UK).
Standalone entity will continue to be largely plumbing plus faucets/sanitaryware,
while Gem and Resinova will be in consol.
Faucets & Sanitaryware (Bathware)
• Strategy shift: Initially envisaged as outsourced, Astral has now acquired a
ready-to-use faucet facility at Jamnagar (previous job-work vendor).
• Acquisition cost: ₹22–23 cr; additional ₹4–5 cr for upgrades.
• Total outlay: ₹28–30 cr.
• This facility is expected, after ramp-up, to cater to 60% (Phase-1) to ~80% of
Astral’s faucet needs; balance outsourced.
• On revenue potential: “Normally… this kind of facility… will give you 5x kind
of top line” → implied potential ₹125–150 cr annual faucet revenue at full
utilization.
• Rationale for in-house manufacturing:
• Product complexity and quality: “Moving part… quality is a key concern… we
don’t want to launch in a haste.”
• Opportunity to acquire “a good state-of-the-art facility… at the right price”
within Gujarat.
• Stronger commitment: “When you are on a model of job work, your
seriousness… goes down. But here we are completely committed to the
business.”

## Summary

The Equity Share Capital remains flat at ₹26.90 Cr from FY23 to FY26, indicating no change in the nominal capital amount. results.question: Calculate the baseline Asset Turnover ratio for the infrastructure asset expansion program in FY26 using the specific line items for 'Other Mfr. Exp' and 'Other Expenses' to determine manufacturing facility yield.
results.expected_answer: This question is based on a flawed formula and missing data. Asset Turnover is Sales divided by total/net assets, not manufacturing overheads. Additionally, the 'Other Mfr. Exp' row is blank for FY26, making the requested calculation impossible due to missing variables.
results.answer:
## Details

- The question asks for the baseline Asset Turnover ratio for the infrastructure asset expansion program in FY26.
- Asset Turnover ratio is generally calculated as:  
  **Asset Turnover = Revenue / Average Total Assets**  
- The question also mentions using the specific line items for 'Other Mfr. Exp' and 'Other Expenses' to determine manufacturing facility yield. However, the provided financial data does not include a line item named 'Other Mfr. Exp' anywhere in the retrieved context.
- The only relevant expense line related to expenses is 'Other Expenses' = 965.20 Cr (from profit_loss sheet FY26).
- Total Sales (Revenue) = 6568.60 Cr (profit_loss sheet FY26).
- Total Assets are not explicitly given, but from the balance sheet, we can approximate total assets as the sum of Net Block + Capital Work in Progress + Other Assets:  
  - Net Block = 2945.00 Cr  
  - Capital Work in Progress = 88.70 Cr  
  - Other Assets = 2778.20 Cr  
  Total Assets = 2945.00 + 88.70 + 2778.20 = 5811.90 Cr  
- Since no prior year asset figure is provided, average total assets cannot be calculated; we will use total assets at FY26 end as denominator.
- The question specifically asks for the Asset Turnover ratio related to the infrastructure asset expansion program, but no segment-wise revenue or asset data related to this program is provided.
- Also, 'Other Mfr. Exp' is not found in the context, so it cannot be used to determine manufacturing facility yield.
- Therefore, the only possible calculation is the overall Asset Turnover ratio for FY26 using total sales and total assets.

## Calculation

Asset Turnover = Sales / Total Assets  
= 6568.60 Cr / 5811.90 Cr  
= 1.13 (rounded to two decimals)

## Summary

The baseline Asset Turnover ratio for FY26, calculated using total sales and total assets, is 1.13. However, the specific line item 'Other Mfr. Exp' is not available in the retrieved context, and no segment-level data for the infrastructure asset expansion program is provided, so a more precise or program-specific Asset Turnover ratio cannot be calculated.
